creator: huntting@glarp.com
text:
I looked over the patch and it seems quite reasonable.

I wont have an opportunity to use fcron on freebsd anytime in the near
future.  Would you like to take over maintaining the port?  If not, I'll ask
on freebsd-ports@


thanks,
brad

On Mon, Jul 18, 2011 at 11:18, Eygene Ryabinkin <rea@freebsd.org> wrote:

> Brad, good day.
>
> Mon, Jul 18, 2011 at 10:55:45AM -0600, Brad Huntting wrote:
> > Alas, I dont have freebsd box I can test this on at the moment.
>
> But will you have one in some near future?  If not, will you maintain
> the port any further?
>
> > If you need me to approve something, let me know what and how.
>
> "what" -- my patch, "how" -- it is a bit harder if you have no
> box to test on.  Well, you can look over my patch and try to
> figure out if it is good or bad.  Another idea is to find someone
> with FreeBSD box who uses fcron and ask him to test the patch.
> Chris is one of the candidates, may be you know other ones.
> Or one can ask for testing in the freebsd-ports@ mailing list.
> --
> Eygene Ryabinkin                                        ,,,^..^,,,
> [ Life's unfair - but root password helps!           | codelabs.ru ]
> [ 82FE 06BC D497 C0DE 49EC  4FF0 16AF 9EAE 8152 ECFB | freebsd.org ]
>
